[Coffee Mate MCP Server](../../../../index.md) / [transport/http/http-transport](../index.md) / startHttpServer

# startHttpServer()

> **Transport Layer**

```ts
function startHttpServer(createServer_: () => McpServer, port: number): Effect<HttpServerHandle>;
```

Defined in: [src/app/transport/http/http-transport.ts:497](https://github.com/EmperorRAG/first-mcp/blob/3d2831b148eaa4c84c57fa062fce6df920f72a50/src/app/transport/http/http-transport.ts#L497)

Creates and starts a raw `node:http` server wired to the MCP Streamable
HTTP transport, returning an [HttpServerHandle](../interfaces/HttpServerHandle.md) for lifecycle
control.

## Parameters

| Parameter       | Type                                                                        | Description                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| --------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `createServer_` | () => [`McpServer`](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/typescript-sdk) | Factory invoked once per MCP `initialize` request to produce a new, fully-configured [McpServer](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/typescript-sdk) instance for that session.          |
| `port`          | `number`                                                                    | TCP port number to listen on. Pass `0` to let the OS assign an ephemeral port (retrieve the actual port via [HttpServerHandle.address](../interfaces/HttpServerHandle.md#property-address)). |

## Returns

[`Effect`](https://effect-ts.github.io/effect/)<[`HttpServerHandle`](../interfaces/HttpServerHandle.md)>

An [Effect.Effect](https://effect-ts.github.io/effect/) that succeeds with an
[HttpServerHandle](../interfaces/HttpServerHandle.md) once the server is listening.

## Remarks

Internally the function:

1. Allocates an Effect [Ref](https://effect-ts.github.io/effect/) holding an empty [TransportMap](#)
   to track active MCP sessions.
2. Creates a `node:http` server whose request listener delegates every
   incoming request to [handleIncoming](#) via
   [Effect.runPromise](https://effect-ts.github.io/effect/).
3. Binds the server to `0.0.0.0` on the given `port` (use `0` for an
   OS-assigned ephemeral port, useful in tests).
4. Logs the listening address via [Effect.logInfo](https://effect-ts.github.io/effect/).

The returned [close()](../interfaces/HttpServerHandle.md#property-close) method performs
graceful shutdown: it iterates all active transports, closes each one,
clears the session map, and finally closes the TCP listener.

## Example

```ts
import { Effect } from "effect";
import { startHttpServer } from "./http-transport.js";

const handle = await Effect.runPromise(
  startHttpServer(() => createMcpServer(), 3001),
);
// … later …
await handle.close();
```
